info.name: TechHire API
info.description:
Production-ready REST API for TechHire — a freemium job board.

This collection covers four canonical request scenarios:
1. Obtain a JWT token (Auth Request)
2. Browse jobs as an unauthenticated user (masked data)
3. Browse jobs as an authenticated Premium user (full data)
4. Fetch a single job posting by ID

Pre-requisite: Run `python manage.py seed_jobs` and create at least one Premium user via the Django admin or shell before running this collection.

Base URL variable: {{base_url}} — set to http://127.0.0.1:8000 for local dev.

variable.description: Root URL of the TechHire API server.
Populated automatically by the 'Obtain JWT Token' request's test script.
Populated automatically by the 'Obtain JWT Token' request's test script.

request.description:
## Auth Request — Obtain JWT Token

Exchanges username + password for a short-lived **access token** (60 minutes) and a long-lived **refresh token** (7 days).

**Expected response (200 OK):**
```json
{
  "access": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...",
  "refresh": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..."
}
```

The test script automatically stores both tokens as collection variables (`access_token`, `refresh_token`). Subsequent requests in this collection use `{{access_token}}`.

response.name: 401 — Invalid Credentials

query.description: Full-text search across title and description.
Sort by created_at descending (newest first). Use created_at for ascending.

request.description:
## Public Request — Masked Data

Fetches the paginated list of job postings **without an Authorization header**.

All users can see `title` and `description`. The three premium fields are masked with the sentinel value `"🔒 Premium Feature"`:
- `company_name`
- `salary_range`
- `application_link`

**Expected response (200 OK):**
```json
{
  "count": 5,
  "next": null,
  "previous": null,
  "results": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "title": "Senior Python Engineer",
      "description": "We are looking for an experienced Python engineer...",
      "location": "San Francisco, CA",
      "company_name": "🔒 Premium Feature",
      "salary_range": "🔒 Premium Feature",
      "application_link": "🔒 Premium Feature",
      "created_at": "2026-03-27T12:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

The same masking applies to authenticated **Basic** members.

exec: pm.test('company_name is masked', () => pm.expect(job.company_name).to.equal('🔒 Premium Feature'));
pm.test('salary_range is masked', () => pm.expect(job.salary_range).to.equal('🔒 Premium Feature'));

response.name: 200 — Masked List
response.body:
{
  "count": 5,
  "next": null,
  "previous": null,
  "results": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "title": "Senior Python Engineer",
      "description": "We are looking for an experienced Python engineer to join our platform team...",
      "location": "San Francisco, CA",
      "company_name": "🔒 Premium Feature",
      "salary_range": "🔒 Premium Feature",
      "application_link": "🔒 Premium Feature",
      "created_at": "2026-03-27T12:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}

header.description: JWT access token for a Premium-tier user. Populated by request #1.

request.description:
## Premium Request — Full Data

Identical endpoint to Request #2, but now the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header is present and the token belongs to a **Premium** user.

All fields are returned unmasked:
- `company_name` → actual company name
- `salary_range` → actual salary band
- `application_link` → actual URL

**Expected response (200 OK):**
```json
{
  "count": 5,
  "next": null,
  "previous": null,
  "results": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "title": "Senior Python Engineer",
      "description": "We are looking for an experienced Python engineer...",
      "location": "San Francisco, CA",
      "company_name": "Anthropic",
      "salary_range": "$160,000 – $220,000",
      "application_link": "https://example.com/jobs/1",
      "created_at": "2026-03-27T12:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

**If the token belongs to a Basic user**, premium fields are still masked despite authentication.

response.name: 200 — Full Data
response.body:
{
  "count": 5,
  "next": null,
  "previous": null,
  "results": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "title": "Senior Python Engineer",
      "description": "We are looking for an experienced Python engineer to join our platform team...",
      "location": "San Francisco, CA",
      "company_name": "Anthropic",
      "salary_range": "$160,000 – $220,000",
      "application_link": "https://example.com/jobs/1",
      "created_at": "2026-03-27T12:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}

header.description: Optional. Omit to test masked response; include to test full response for Premium users.

request.description:
## Detail Request — Single Job Record

Fetches a single `JobPosting` by its primary key (`/api/jobs/<id>/`).

The same field-level masking applies:
- **No token / Basic token** → premium fields masked
- **Premium token** → all fields revealed

**Expected response with Premium token (200 OK):**
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "title": "Senior Python Engineer",
  "description": "We are looking for an experienced Python engineer to join our platform team...",
  "location": "San Francisco, CA",
  "company_name": "Anthropic",
  "salary_range": "$160,000 – $220,000",
  "application_link": "https://example.com/jobs/1",
  "created_at": "2026-03-27T12:00:00Z"
}
```

**404 response** if the ID does not exist:
```json
{"detail": "No JobPosting matches the given query."}
```

response.name: 200 — Single Job (Premium user)
response.body:
{
  "id": 1,
  "title": "Senior Python Engineer",
  "description": "We are looking for an experienced Python engineer to join our platform team that handles millions of requests daily.",
  "location": "San Francisco, CA",
  "company_name": "Anthropic",
  "salary_range": "$160,000 – $220,000",
  "application_link": "https://example.com/jobs/1",
  "created_at": "2026-03-27T12:00:00Z"
}

response.name: 200 — Single Job (Unauthenticated)
response.body:
{
  "id": 1,
  "title": "Senior Python Engineer",
  "description": "We are looking for an experienced Python engineer to join our platform team that handles millions of requests daily.",
  "location": "San Francisco, CA",
  "company_name": "🔒 Premium Feature",
  "salary_range": "🔒 Premium Feature",
  "application_link": "🔒 Premium Feature",
  "created_at": "2026-03-27T12:00:00Z"
}

request.description:
Uses the refresh token to obtain a new access token without re-authenticating.

Because `ROTATE_REFRESH_TOKENS = True` and `BLACKLIST_AFTER_ROTATION = True` in settings, each refresh also rotates and blacklists the old refresh token.

Returns the authenticated user's membership tier.

**Expected response (200 OK):**
```json
{
  "id": 2,
  "username": "premium_user",
  "email": "premium@example.com",
  "membership_type": "Premium"
}
```

Returns `401` if no valid token is supplied.
